summary: Agentic P&L execution layer for enterprise spend and revenue leakage.
editor_note: The bet is that enterprise AI should recover money, not summarize meetings. Rivvun connects contract, invoice, procurement, CRM, and ERP evidence, then runs 2 agent families for Spend Assurance and Margin Defense with governed fixes and audit trails. Unlike post-quarter finance forensics, it tries to catch rebates, pricing gaps, and settlement issues before they become write-offs. The risk is proving autonomous corrections across messy enterprise policies.

product_evidence.claim: The official site positions Rivvun as an agentic execution layer for detecting spend leakage, margin erosion, and revenue leakage.
Rivvun describes Spend Assurance and Margin Defense agents for invoice assessment, rebates, pricing enforcement, and commitments.
The official product surface cites outcomes including $70M spend reduction identified and 300% ROI on royalty governance.
The careers page confirms an official hiring surface without requiring VentureDex to scrape roles, locations, or job counts.

market_context.primary_user: Finance, procurement, commercial, and revenue operations teams managing contract, invoice, pricing, and settlement controls.
market_context.differentiation: Rivvun focuses agents on recoverable P&L leakage and governed corrective action instead of finance summaries.
market_context.why_now: Large companies have enough operational data for AI agents to inspect spend and margin leaks, but still need audit trails for corrections.

risks.claim: The open question is whether autonomous corrections can work across inconsistent policies, supplier data, and enterprise approval rules.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
